script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah. Today is June 27th, 2026. The Daily Tanu-chan Show is on.
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。今日は2026年6月27日。ザ・デイリー・タヌちゃんショーの時間です。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Good morning, Alex. It's really coming down out there, isn't it? Heavy rain all morning.
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。外は本当に降ってるわね。午前中ずっと土砂降りだわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, it feels like this whole week has been like this. And the air is so humid. At least it's not super hot, I guess. Only 23 degrees Celsius.
script.ja: ええ、今週ずっとこんな感じですよね。空気もすごく湿っぽい。まあ、めちゃくちゃ暑くないのが救いかな。23度だし。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: True. Better than a sweltering typhoon, I suppose. More tea for me, please.
script.ja: そうね。うだるような台風よりはマシだわ、きっと。私はもう少しお茶を。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I really need my coffee today. My little one was up three times last night. He's a year old now, so he's on the move all the time.
script.ja: 僕は今日、コーヒーが本当に必要です。うちの子、昨夜3回も起きて。もう1歳だから、ずっと動き回ってるんです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That's the age, isn't it? My son was like that. Always exploring.
script.ja: そういう時期よね。うちの息子もそうだったわ。いつも探検してるの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, exploring everything. Anyway, did you see the news headlines? This one sounds interesting: 'Two-day event will bring unique Japan-inspired steampunk style to Harajuku.'
script.ja: ええ、何でも探検するんです。ところで、ニュースの見出し見ました？これ面白そう。『2日間のイベントで、日本独自の着想を得たスチームパンクスタイルが原宿に登場』。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: I saw that. It said steampunk generally 'incorporates 19th century industrial technology and Victorian aesthetics'.
script.ja: 見たわ。スチームパンクは一般的に「19世紀の産業技術とヴィクトリア朝の美学を取り入れる」って書いてあったわね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, 'incorporates'. What does that word mean? I see it sometimes but I'm not sure.
script.ja: ああ、「incorporates」。その言葉、どういう意味ですか？時々見かけるんですけど、よく分からなくて。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It means to include something as part of a larger whole. Like making it a component.
script.ja: 何かをより大きな全体の一部として含める、という意味よ。構成要素にするような感じね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, I see. So the event will 'incorporate' Japanese style into steampunk. That makes sense. I wouldn't mind checking it out, actually. Harajuku is always interesting.
script.ja: なるほど。だから、イベントはスチームパンクに日本のスタイルを「取り入れる」わけですね。納得です。実際、ちょっと見に行ってもいいかも。原宿はいつも面白いですし。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's definitely unique. There was another headline about a sword belonging to one of Japan’s last samurai, Yoshida Shoin, found in America.
script.ja: 確かにユニークね。他には、日本の最後の侍の一人、吉田松陰の刀がアメリカで見つかったという見出しがあったわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Wow, a real samurai sword. That's pretty amazing, isn't it? To find something like that so far from home.
script.ja: わあ、本物の侍の刀ですか。すごいですね。そんなものが故郷から遠く離れた場所で見つかるなんて。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is. That historical connection, it's quite a story. My daughter actually studied Yoshida Shoin in school.
script.ja: そうね。その歴史的なつながり、なかなか物語があるわね。うちの娘も学校で吉田松陰について勉強したのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, I didn't know that. I guess I need to brush up on my history. Speaking of unique, there's Keisuke Unosawa, 'making the ordinary extraordinary'.
script.ja: ああ、知りませんでした。歴史の勉強し直さないとですね。ユニークと言えば、宇野澤啓介さん、『日常を非日常にする』っていう記事がありましたね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, I like that. Finding beauty in everyday objects. It's a nice perspective to have.
script.ja: ええ、あれいいわね。日常の物の中に美しさを見出す。そういう視点を持つのは素晴らしいことよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, I guess so. My office needs a bit of that. Making 'paperwork' extraordinary. That's not going to change at my office, though.
script.ja: ええ、そうですね。うちのオフィスもそういうのが少し必要かも。「書類仕事」を非日常にする、とか。でも、うちのオフィスでは無理だろうな。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Probably not.
script.ja: 多分ね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You know, Alex, tomorrow is actually 貿易記念日, or Trade Memorial Day.
script.ja: ねえ、アレックス、明日って実は貿易記念日なのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, really? I didn't know that was a thing. What happened on that day?
script.ja: ああ、そうなんですか？そんな日があるなんて知りませんでした。その日は何があったんですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Back in 1859, on June 28th, the Tokugawa shogunate issued a 'decree' permitting free trade in Yokohama, Nagasaki, and Hakodate.
script.ja: 1859年の6月28日に、徳川幕府が横浜、長崎、箱館で自由貿易を許可する「布告」を出した日なの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: A 'decree'? What's that? Like a formal announcement?
script.ja: 「布告」ですか？それって何ですか？公式な発表みたいな？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Exactly. It's a formal order or law, usually from a ruler or government. It really opened Japan to the world.
script.ja: その通りよ。通常、統治者や政府が出す正式な命令や法律のことね。あれが日本を世界に開いたのよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Wow, so that's a pretty big deal historically. Yokohama, Nagasaki... I've been to Nagasaki. It's a beautiful city.
script.ja: へえ、それは歴史的にかなり大きな出来事ですね。横浜、長崎... 長崎には行ったことがあります。美しい街ですよね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is. The history there is so rich. I'd love to go back sometime, maybe visit a quiet hot spring resort nearby, like Unzen. I hear it's lovely.
script.ja: ええ。あの街の歴史は本当に豊かだわ。いつかまた行きたいわね、近くの静かな温泉地、雲仙とか。素敵らしいわよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Unzen, huh? Sounds relaxing. I could use a trip like that. Away from baby alarms and... remote controls.
script.ja: 雲仙ですか？リラックスできそうですね。そういう旅行、僕も必要かも。赤ちゃんの目覚まし時計と...リモコンから離れて。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Mmm, I'm sure.
script.ja: ええ、きっとね。

news.title: Two-day event will bring unique Japan-inspired steampunk style to Harajuku
news.summary: While the steampunk style generally incorporates 19th century industrial technology and Victorian aesthetics, Japan’s largest steampunk event, Steam Garden, showcases the fashion with its own unique twist. Drawing…

news.title: Sword of one of Japan’s last samurai discovered in house in America
news.summary: Born in 1830, Yoshida Shoin was part of Japan’s last generation of samurai. While that distinction carries with it a certain tragically romantic feel, the truth is that…

news.title: Making the ordinary extraordinary with Keisuke Unosawa
news.summary: The story of Keisuke Unosawa’s design career is far from ordinary. You won’t find skills developed at a prestigious design school or an expensive intern program abroad; there…

wikipedia.title: 貿易記念日
wikipedia.description: 1859年5月28日（新暦6月28日）、徳川幕府がアメリカ、イギリス、フランス、ロシア、オランダと結んだ友好通商条約に基づき、横浜、長崎、箱館（函館）で自由貿易許可を布告。
